[[Category:Cases]]
The '''Patient case''' marks the object of transitive verbs and the subject of stative verbs in Aelaki. It is the least morphologically marked case, having evolved from an earlier ergative case system.

== Formation ==

The Patient case is the least marked of the five Aelaki cases, reflecting its historical origin as the default (absolutive) case in the language's earlier ergative alignment. It serves a dual function:

* '''Object''' of transitive verbs (the entity acted upon)
* '''Subject''' of stative verbs (the entity in a state)

== Declension ==

Demonstrated with the feminine singular noun '''basol''' ("tree"):

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Form
|-
| 1st person || Basoloth
|-
| 2nd person || Basoloj
|-
| 3rd person || Basolosh
|-
| 4th person || Basol
|-
| Inanimate || Basïlïf
|}

Note that the 4th person form is identical to the bare noun stem, and the inanimate form uses the distinctive vowel harmony pattern with ''ï'' and the ''-ïf'' suffix.

== Case Hierarchy ==

The Patient case occupies the middle position in the case hierarchy for verb agreement:

[[Agent]] > [[Dative]] > '''Patient''' > [[Instrumental]] > [[Possessive]] > [[Speaker]]

== See Also ==

* [[Agent]] - marks the subject of transitive/active verbs
* [[Instrumental]] - marks the instrument, companion, or location
* [[Dative]] - marks the recipient or beneficiary
* [[Speaker]] - marks the source of reported information
* [[Possessive]] - marks genitive constructions
